name: Kanji
description: Kanji is a traditional Indian drink made from fermented black carrots. It is a rich source of probiotics, vitamins, and minerals. It is a cooling and refreshing drink that is perfect for summer.
ingredients: 500g Black Carrot (or 350 g Carrot + 150 g Beetroot as substitute)
process: Boil water and let it cool
Chop the black carrots into middle-sized pieces
Add chopped black carrots and mustard seeds to the jar
Add red chili powder and salt to the jar
Pour the cooled water into the jar and mix well
Let it ferment for 3 days
Serve chilled

name: Saktu
description: Sattu is a traditional Indian drink made from roasted barley flour. It is a rich source of protein, fiber, and other essential nutrients. It is a cooling and refreshing drink that is perfect for summer.
ingredients: 1 glass Water or Milk (room temperature or lukewarm)
1–2 tsp Jaggery (Gur) or Honey (avoid honey if using hot milk)
process: Place 2–3 tbsp of barley flour into a tall glass or deep bowl. Add only about 2 tablespoons of liquid first.
Mix the flour and small amount of liquid into a thick, smooth paste – this prevents lumps.
Add Jaggery and Cardamom powder. If using Ghee, add it now. Mix well.
Slowly pour in the rest of the water or milk. Whisk vigorously until frothy.
Note: If using Honey, add it last and only if the liquid is not hot.
Drink it immediately as barley will settle. Keep a spoon handy to stir while sipping.
Can be refrigerated and reheated gently.
healthBenefits: Handles excess kapha and pitta doshas
classicalReference: Classification of foods - anna panavidhi - Charaka Samhita
Dietary Preparations including saktu - Sushruta Samhita

name: Gongura Pappu
description: A traditional Andhra Pradesh dal made with tangy gongura (sorrel leaves) and protein-rich toor dal. Known for its distinct sour taste and comforting nature, it is commonly served with hot rice and ghee as a staple meal.
process: Wash and pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until soft, then mash it
Cook gongura leaves with green chilies until wilted and mash coarsely
Combine gongura with dal, add onion, tomato, and salt
Simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes
Prepare tempering with mustard seeds, cumin seeds, garlic, red chilies, curry leaves, and hing
Pour tempering over the dal and mix well before serving
healthBenefits: Rich in iron and vitamin C from gongura
Provides plant-based protein from toor dal
Supports digestion due to sour (amla) taste
classicalReference: Amla Rasa (Sour Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita
Rasa-Dosha Relationship (Amla increases Pitta) - Charaka Samhita
Ahara Varga Classification (Bhojyam foods) - Charaka Samhita
Shimbi Dhanya (Legumes like Toor Dal) - Charaka Samhita

name: Neem Flower Rasam
description: A traditional South Indian rasam made using neem flowers, known for its characteristic bitter taste. Commonly prepared during Tamil New Year, it symbolizes the acceptance of all flavors of life and supports digestion.
process: Dry roast neem flowers in ghee until aromatic and slightly dark
Prepare tamarind extract and bring it to a boil
Add crushed pepper and cumin, salt, and jaggery
Simmer for 5-7 minutes
Add roasted neem flowers and cook briefly
Prepare tempering with mustard seeds, red chilies, curry leaves, and hing
Add tempering to rasam and serve hot
classicalReference: Tikta Rasa (Bitter Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita
Rasa-Dosha Relationship (Tikta reduces Pitta and Kapha) - Charaka Samhita
Ahara Classification (Paaniyam - liquid foods) - Charaka Samhita
Krimighna (Antimicrobial properties of bitter substances) - Charaka Samhita

name: Kutaja Phanta
description: Kutaja Phanta is a traditional Ayurvedic herbal infusion made from the bark of the Kutaja plant. It is primarily used for its digestive and anti-diarrheal properties and is commonly recommended in classical Ayurvedic treatments.
process: Add coarsely powdered kutaja bark to a cup
Pour hot water over it
Cover and let it steep for 10-15 minutes
Strain and consume warm
healthBenefits: Effective in managing diarrhea and dysentery
classicalReference: Kashaya Rasa (Astringent Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita
Rasa-Dosha Relationship (Kashaya reduces Pitta and Kapha) - Charaka Samhita
Phanta Kalpana (Herbal Infusion Preparation) - Charaka Samhita

name: Kumari Kuber Paak
description: A traditional Ayurvedic sweet made from aloe vera pulp, known for its rejuvenating and digestive properties. It is rich, nourishing, and often consumed in small quantities.
process: Extract and clean aloe vera pulp
Roast wheat flour in ghee until aromatic
Add aloe pulp and cook until moisture reduces
Add sugar and mix well
Cook until thick consistency forms
Add cardamom and nuts, then cool and serve
classicalReference: Madhura Rasa (Sweet Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita

name: Lau Khar
description: A traditional Assamese dish made with bottle gourd and khar (alkaline extract). It is light, cleansing, and known for aiding digestion.
process: Heat mustard oil in a pan
Add green chilies and sauté
Add bottle gourd and cook until soft
Add diluted kola khar and salt
Cook for a few minutes and serve
classicalReference: Pathya Ahara (Light and digestible foods) - Charaka Samhita

name: Pathrode
description: A traditional steamed dish made from colocasia leaves layered with a spiced rice and dal paste. It has a unique blend of flavors and is rich in nutrients.
process: Soak rice and dal, then grind with spices
Prepare a thick paste with tamarind and jaggery
Spread paste on colocasia leaves and layer them
Roll tightly and steam for 30-40 minutes
Cool, slice, and optionally shallow fry
classicalReference: Katu Rasa (Pungent Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita
Rasa-Dosha Relationship (Katu increases Vata) - Charaka Samhita

name: Mandia Peja
description: A traditional ragi-based porridge from Odisha, known for its cooling properties and high nutritional value. It is commonly consumed as a light, hydrating meal.
process: Mix ragi flour with water to avoid lumps
Cook on low flame, stirring continuously
Cook until thick and smooth
Add salt and cool slightly
Mix buttermilk if desired and serve
classicalReference: Madhura Rasa (Sweet Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita
Pathya Ahara (Light and nourishing foods) - Charaka Samhita
Dhanya Varga (Cereals like ragi) - Charaka Samhita

name: Navara Rice Kanji
description: A medicinal porridge from Kerala made using Navara rice, known in Ayurveda for its healing and strengthening properties. Often used in therapeutic diets.
process: Wash and cook navara rice in water until soft
Add milk and simmer further
Cook until porridge consistency is achieved
Serve warm
classicalReference: Madhura Rasa (Sweet Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita

name: Anapakaya Ginjalapodi
description: A traditional Andhra spice powder made from bottle gourd seeds, known for its nutty flavor and digestive benefits. Typically eaten mixed with rice or as a side accompaniment.
process: Dry roast bottle gourd seeds until aromatic
Roast red chilies, cumin, and garlic in oil
Cool and grind all ingredients to a coarse powder
Store in an airtight container
classicalReference: Katu Rasa (Pungent Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita
Rasa-Dosha Relationship (Katu increases Vata) - Charaka Samhita

name: Mahua Flower Laddu
description: A traditional tribal sweet made from dried mahua flowers, valued for its natural sweetness and energy-boosting properties.
process: Clean and lightly roast mahua flowers
Roast flour in ghee
Mix mahua, jaggery, and flour
Shape into laddus
Cool and serve
classicalReference: Madhura Rasa (Sweet Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita

name: Tikhur Kheer
description: A cooling dessert made from arrowroot powder, popular in central India. It is light, soothing, and easy to digest.
process: Mix tikhur powder with a little water to avoid lumps
Heat milk and add the mixture
Cook while stirring until thick
Add sugar and cardamom
Garnish with nuts and serve
classicalReference: Madhura Rasa (Sweet Taste) Properties - Charaka Samhita

name: Kokum Drink (Vrikshamla)
description: A refreshing summer drink made from kokum fruit, known for its cooling and digestive properties. Widely consumed in coastal regions.
process: Soak kokum in water for 10 minutes
Mash to extract flavor
Strain and add jaggery, cumin, and salt
Mix well and serve chilled

name: Krushara
description: An ancient Ayurvedic dish made from rice and lentils, similar to khichdi. It is nourishing, easy to digest, and commonly recommended in therapeutic diets.
process: Wash rice and green gram
Cook together with water until soft
Add ghee and salt
Mix well and serve warm

name: Vilepi
description: A thick rice gruel described in Ayurveda, used as a nourishing and easily digestible food, especially during illness or recovery.
process: Wash rice thoroughly
Cook with water until thick consistency is achieved
Add salt and serve warm

name: Parpat
description: A traditional thin, crispy accompaniment similar to papad, made from lentil flour and spices, often served with meals.
process: Prepare dough using flour and spices
Roll into thin discs
Dry under sunlight
Roast or fry before serving

name: Shashkuli
description: An ancient deep-fried sweet preparation mentioned in classical texts, made using rice flour and jaggery, often prepared during festive occasions.
process: Prepare jaggery syrup
Mix with rice flour to form dough
Shape into coils or rings
Deep fry until golden
Cool and serve

name: Panak
description: A traditional herbal drink made during festivals like Rama Navami, known for its cooling and digestive properties.
process: Dissolve jaggery in water
Add dry ginger, pepper, and cardamom
Mix well and strain if needed
Serve fresh

name: Modak
description: A popular sweet dumpling offered during festivals, especially Ganesh Chaturthi. It is filled with coconut and jaggery mixture.
process: Prepare filling with coconut and jaggery
Make dough from rice flour
Shape into dumplings with filling
Steam until cooked
Serve warm

name: Tangedu Flower Rasam
description: A traditional rasam made from tangedu flowers, commonly associated with regional festivals and known for its medicinal properties.
process: Boil tamarind extract with spices
Add cleaned tangedu flowers
Simmer briefly
Prepare tempering and add
Serve hot
classicalReference: Rasa-Dosha Relationship (Kashaya reduces Kapha) - Charaka Samhita
